title: Pack address into upper bits with shl(96) for reuse across multiple slot computations
trigger_patterns: same address used in multiple slot computations
from address used for both balance and allowance slot
description: Shifting an address left by 96 bits (shl(96, addr)) packs it into the upper 160 bits of a uint256. This packed value can then be OR-ed with different seeds to derive multiple different slots without re-mstore-ing the address each time.
when_to_apply: Functions that derive multiple storage slots from the same address
when_not_to_apply: Single slot derivation where packing adds complexity without benefit
